page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc8i.6xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6462C processor, running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. With a thread-per-core ratio of 1.0, each vCPU receives a dedicated physical core without hyperthreading, ensuring consistent compute execution.

The instance is configured with 48.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for operating system and application data. Network connectivity is provisioned with an 18 Gbps baseline bandwidth. There are no integrated graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance type.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated core allocation and high clock speed prioritize raw compute throughput over memory capacity. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require large in-memory datasets or local NVMe storage. Suitable workloads include batch processing, web serving, distributed analytics nodes, and CPU-intensive compilation tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc8i.6xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 48.0 GB of RAM on the x86_64 architecture. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6462C processor running at 3.3 GHz under the KVM hypervisor, the instance allocates one physical core per vCPU with a thread-per-core ratio of 1.0. This configuration provides consistent compute performance without hyperthreading overhead. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, relying on external network storage and offering an 18 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. With a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio, this server is designed for compute-heavy tasks rather than memory-intensive operations. It is suited for workloads such as batch processing, web serving, and distributed compute nodes.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores, a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and 18 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
